Simulation visualisations

2026-06-05

Prep

Loading data

experiment_results <- list()

experiment_results[["inflation_factor_2"]] <- readRDS("/data/gpfs/projects/punim2745/03.CUTRUN_normalisation_KA/Henikoff_simulations/R_analysis_final/05.Artificially_inflate/results/inflation_factor_2/experiment_summary.rds")

experiment_results[["inflation_factor_5"]] <- readRDS("/data/gpfs/projects/punim2745/03.CUTRUN_normalisation_KA/Henikoff_simulations/R_analysis_final/05.Artificially_inflate/results/inflation_factor_5/experiment_summary.rds")

experiment_results[["inflation_factor_10"]] <- readRDS("/data/gpfs/projects/punim2745/03.CUTRUN_normalisation_KA/Henikoff_simulations/R_analysis_final/05.Artificially_inflate/results/inflation_factor_10/experiment_summary.rds")

experiment_results[["inflation_factor_20"]]<- readRDS("/data/gpfs/projects/punim2745/03.CUTRUN_normalisation_KA/Henikoff_simulations/R_analysis_final/05.Artificially_inflate/results/inflation_factor_20/experiment_summary.rds")

Getting metrics

experiment_summary <- data.frame(rand_comp =paste0(experiment_results[["inflation_factor_5"]]$groupB_samples, "_vs_", experiment_results[["inflation_factor_5"]]$groupA_samples) %>% gsub("CTCF_", "",.) %>% gsub(" ","",.))



norm_methods <- c("LS", "RLE_RiP", "RLE_BG", "greenlist") ## Excluding greenlist
inflation_factors <- names(experiment_results)
plotting_order <- c()
counter = 1
for(norm_method in norm_methods){
  for(inflation_factor in inflation_factors){
    inflation_factor_num = inflation_factor %>% gsub("inflation_factor","",.)

    experiment_summary[,paste0(norm_method,"_sensitivity",inflation_factor_num)] <- experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_TP")]/(experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_TP")]+experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_FN")]) * 100
  experiment_summary[,paste0(norm_method,"_specificity",inflation_factor_num)] <- experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_TN")]/(experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_FP")]+experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_TN")]) * 100
experiment_summary[,paste0(norm_method,"_FDR",inflation_factor_num)] <- experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_FP")]/(experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_FP")]+experiment_results[[inflation_factor]][,paste0(norm_method,"_I_TP")]) * 100
    
plotting_order[counter] <- paste0(norm_method,inflation_factor_num)
counter = counter +1
    
  }
  
}


colnames(experiment_summary) <- colnames(experiment_summary) %>% gsub("LS","Library size",.) %>% gsub("greenlist", "Greenlist",.) %>% gsub("RLE_RiP","Reads in peak",.) %>% gsub("RLE_BG", "Background", .)

Sensitivity plots

experiment_summary_sensitivity <- experiment_summary[,grep("sensitivity",colnames(experiment_summary))]
colnames(experiment_summary_sensitivity) <- gsub("sensitivity_","",colnames(experiment_summary_sensitivity))
experiment_summary_sensitivity_mat <- experiment_summary_sensitivity %>% as.matrix()
rownames(experiment_summary_sensitivity_mat) <- paste(rownames(experiment_summary),experiment_summary$rand_comp, sep = ". ")
experiment_summary_sensitivity[experiment_summary_sensitivity=="NaN"] <- c(-1)
experiment_summary_sensitivity <-experiment_summary_sensitivity[,!grepl("_2$", colnames(experiment_summary_sensitivity))]
experiment_summary_sensitivity_blocks <- data.frame(NaN_run =colSums(experiment_summary_sensitivity<0) , 
                                                    less_than_80 = colSums(experiment_summary_sensitivity>=0 & experiment_summary_sensitivity<80), 
                                                    between_80_95 = colSums(experiment_summary_sensitivity>80 & experiment_summary_sensitivity<95), 
                                                    greater_95 = colSums(experiment_summary_sensitivity>95))

experiment_summary_sensitivity_blocks$method <- rownames(experiment_summary_sensitivity_blocks)


# Reshape data to long format for ggplot
df_long <- experiment_summary_sensitivity_blocks %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -method, 
               names_to = "category", 
               values_to = "count")
df_long <- df_long %>% separate(method, into = c("norm_method", "inflation_factor"), sep="_", remove = FALSE)

# Convert category to factor with desired order
df_long$category <- factor(df_long$category, 
                           levels = c("NaN_run","less_than_80","between_80_95","greater_95" ))

df_long$norm_method <- factor(df_long$norm_method, levels = c( "Library size" ,  "Background", "Greenlist", "Reads in peak" ))
df_long$inflation_factor <- factor(df_long$inflation_factor, levels = c("5","10","20"))

Figure generation - Stacked bar plot

sensitivity_stacked_bar <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = inflation_factor, y = count, fill = category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~norm_method, scales="free", nrow=1) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("greater_95" = "#0d7d87",
                                "between_80_95" = "#99c6cc", 
                                "less_than_80" = "#F44336"),
                    labels = c("<80","80-95", "> 95"),
                    name = "% Sensitivity") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 30, by = 5)) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
        legend.position = "right") +
  labs(title = "Inflation experiment - Sensitivity",
       x = "Inflation factor",
       y = "Run")

sensitivity_stacked_bar

pdf("figures_pdf/sensitivity_stacked_bar.pdf", width = 6, height = 3)
sensitivity_stacked_bar
dev.off()
## png 
##   2

FDR

experiment_summary_FDR<- experiment_summary[,grep("FDR",colnames(experiment_summary))]
colnames(experiment_summary_FDR) <- gsub("FDR_","",colnames(experiment_summary_FDR))

experiment_summary_FDR_mat <- experiment_summary_FDR %>% as.matrix()
rownames(experiment_summary_FDR_mat) <- paste(rownames(experiment_summary),experiment_summary$rand_comp, sep = ". ")
experiment_summary_FDR[experiment_summary_FDR=="NaN"] <- c(-1)
experiment_summary_FDR <-experiment_summary_FDR[,!grepl("_2$", colnames(experiment_summary_FDR))]


experiment_summary_FDR_blocks <- data.frame(NaN_run =colSums(experiment_summary_FDR<0) , 
                                            less_than_five = colSums(experiment_summary_FDR>=0 &experiment_summary_FDR<5),
                                            between_five_ten = colSums(experiment_summary_FDR>5&experiment_summary_FDR<10),
                                            greater_ten = colSums(experiment_summary_FDR>10))
                           

experiment_summary_FDR_blocks$method <- rownames(experiment_summary_FDR_blocks)

df <- experiment_summary_FDR_blocks
# Reshape data to long format for ggplot
df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -method, 
               names_to = "category", 
               values_to = "count")
df_long <- df_long %>% separate(method, into = c("norm_method", "inflation_factor"), sep="_", remove = FALSE)

# Convert category to factor with desired order
df_long$category <- factor(df_long$category, 
                           levels = c("NaN_run","greater_ten",  "between_five_ten","less_than_five"))
df_long$method <- factor(df_long$method, levels = unique(df_long$method))
df_long$norm_method <- factor(df_long$norm_method, levels = c( "Library size" ,  "Background", "Greenlist", "Reads in peak" ))

df_long$inflation_factor <- factor(df_long$inflation_factor, levels = c("5","10","20"))

df_long$method <- factor(df_long$method, levels = unique(df_long$method))

Figure generation - Stacked bar plot

FDR_stacked_bar <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = inflation_factor, y = count, fill = category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity",color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
 facet_wrap(~norm_method, scales="free", nrow=1) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("less_than_five" = "#0d7d87",
                                "between_five_ten" = "#99c6cc", 
                                "greater_ten" = "#F44336",
                               "NaN_run" = "grey"),
                    labels = c("NaN" ,"> 10",  "5-10","< 5"),
                    name = "% FDR") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 30, by = 5)) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
        legend.position = "right") +
  labs(title = "Inflation experiment - False Discovery Rate",
       x = "Inflation factor",
       y = "Run")

FDR_stacked_bar

pdf("figures_pdf/FDR_stacked_bar.pdf", width = 6, height = 3)
FDR_stacked_bar
dev.off()
## png 
##   2
